blocks.body: y = mx + b, where m is the slope and b is the y-intercept (the y-value where the line crosses the y-axis).
To graph y = mx + b: (1) Plot the y-intercept (0, b). (2) Use the slope m = rise/run to find the next point. (3) Draw the line through both points.

blocks.label: Example 1
blocks.body: Graph y = (2/3)x - 4. y-intercept: (0, -4). Slope 2/3 means up 2, right 3: next point is (3, -2). Draw the line.
To write the equation from slope and y-intercept: substitute m and b directly into y = mx + b. Example: slope = -1/2, y-intercept = 3. Equation: y = -1/2 x + 3.

blocks.label: Example 2
blocks.body: Write equation of line with slope 4 passing through (0, -7). y = 4x - 7.
Any linear equation can be rewritten in slope-intercept form by solving for y. Example: 3x + 2y = 8 -> 2y = -3x + 8 -> y = -3/2 x + 4. Now slope = -3/2, y-intercept = 4.
